evals.name: open-pr-inline-review
evals.prompt: do a deep review of PR #6 in this repo
evals.expected_output: A GitHub review POSTED on PR #6 via one reviews API call (event: COMMENT): inline comments anchored to diff lines for specific findings (each prefixed with bold severity), plus a summary comment with verdict, claims audit, verification log, and not-reviewed scope. The chat reply is just the review URL and a one-line verdict. No local report files. Never APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES.
expectations: A review is actually posted on the PR (verifiable via gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/6/reviews) with event COMMENT, not APPROVE or REQUEST_CHANGES
Findings are inline comments anchored to lines present in the PR diff, each starting with a bold severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low)
The summary comment contains a verdict, a claims-audit treatment (verified vs unverified), local verification results, and an explicit not-reviewed scope section
The review does not duplicate points already made by existing comments on the same lines
No local report file is produced; the final chat message is essentially the review URL plus a one-line verdict
Spot-check accuracy: sampled inline comments match the actual code at the anchored locations (no fabricated findings)

evals.name: no-pr-stops-cleanly
evals.expected_output: When the current branch has no open PR, the skill determines that, says so, suggests opening a PR first, and stops — it does not improvise a local branch review, does not hallucinate a PR number, and posts nothing.
expectations: Correctly determines that no PR exists for the current branch and says so explicitly
Suggests gh pr create (or equivalent) rather than improvising a local review
Posts no comments or reviews to any PR
Does not fabricate a PR number, PR comments, or CI results
